title: Weak Cryptographic Hash Function
description: MD5 and SHA-1 are cryptographically broken and must not be used for integrity verification, digital signatures, or any security purpose (OWASP A02:2021 / CWE-327).

finding.message: Weak hash function '{{token}}' used in {{file}} at line {{line}}.

references: Use SHA-256, SHA-384, or SHA-512 for general-purpose hashing.
Use SHA-3 or BLAKE3 for new implementations requiring collision resistance.
MD5/SHA-1 are still acceptable for non-security purposes (checksums, deduplication) but never for security.
NIST formally deprecated SHA-1 for digital signatures (NIST SP 800-131A Rev 2, 2022).
